findings.why: This gene is linked to BMI changes even in physically active people.
findings.plan_b: Consult a nutritionist for tailored diet.
findings.survival_tip: Gene PRDX4P1 - THAP12P9 may affect your BMI even if you exercise.

findings.why: This gene strongly influences BMI when physical activity is low.
findings.plan_a: Increase daily step count to at least 10,000.
findings.survival_tip: Lack of exercise triggers the effect of gene RNU4-17P - MC4R on your weight.

findings.why: This gene interacts with lifestyle factors like smoking to affect BMI.
findings.plan_a: Avoid smoking and maintain a clean diet.
findings.survival_tip: Gene RPTOR affects BMI, especially when combined with other habits.

findings.why: This gene affects how your body processes carbohydrates and insulin sensitivity.
findings.plan_a: Limit simple carbs and focus on complex carbs.
findings.survival_tip: Gene TCF7L2 strongly influences carb metabolism. Watch your intake.

findings.why: This gene affects the transport and binding of Vitamin D in your blood.
findings.survival_tip: You may have a genetic predisposition to lower Vitamin D levels.

findings.why: This gene is associated with a higher risk of developing hearing loss with age.
findings.survival_tip: Protect your ears from loud noises to preserve hearing longer.

findings.why: This gene makes the inner ear more sensitive to damage from loud sounds.
findings.survival_tip: Your baseline risk is typical, but protection is always recommended.

findings.why: This gene influences baseline liver enzyme levels, reflecting liver stress.
findings.plan_a: Limit alcohol consumption and processed sugars.
findings.plan_b: Milk thistle supplementation after doctor consultation.
findings.survival_tip: Gene PNPLA3 is linked to fatty liver risk. Keep a clean diet.

findings.organ: Organ Health: Chronic kidney disease risk
findings.why: This gene is linked to the filtration capacity of the kidneys over time.
findings.plan_a: Maintain healthy blood pressure and hydration.
findings.survival_tip: Hydration is key to supporting your kidneys with this genetic profile.
